articles.title: Window Seal Failure — Diagnosis, Warranty Process & Replacement Guide
articles.description: Step-by-step guide for diagnosing window seal failure, submitting warranty claims, and ordering replacement parts from Andersen and Pella.
articles.content:
Window Seal Failure — Diagnosis & Warranty Process

Symptoms

  • Visible water intrusion around window frame or sill
  • Condensation between glass panes (failed IG unit)
  • Visible cracking, gaps, or compression failure in perimeter seal
  • Drafts around closed window

Diagnostic Steps

  1. Inspect exterior perimeter seal — look for cracking, separation, or compression failure
  2. Apply light water spray (garden hose) around exterior frame while second person monitors interior for intrusion
  3. Check installation date — if under 2 years, likely covered under manufacturer warranty
  4. Take photos of damage location and extent before disturbing
  5. Document serial number from manufacturer sticker (usually on corner of sash or frame)

Warranty Determination

  • Under 2 years: Submit manufacturer warranty claim — use PART-SEAL-KIT from manufacturer, no charge to customer
  • 2-10 years: Review installation warranty — may be contractor-installed issue vs manufacturer defect. Assess cause before determining coverage.
  • Over 10 years: Standard wear — customer pay, quote replacement

Parts Order

For standard casement seal replacement, order PART-SEAL-KIT from Andersen or Pella manufacturer portal. Lead time typically 10-14 business days for standard product, up to 3-4 weeks for custom sizing.

Proactive Customer Communication

Set a 1-week automated check-in reminder when parts lead time exceeds 5 business days. Do not wait for customer to call — send proactive status update SMS at Day 7 and upon parts arrival.

articles.title: Sliding Window / Patio Door Operator Mechanism — Replacement Procedure
articles.description: How to diagnose and replace the operator mechanism on Pella and Andersen sliding windows and patio doors. Includes parts lookup and labor time estimates.
articles.content:
Sliding Window Operator Mechanism Replacement

Symptoms

  • Window handle spins freely without engaging
  • Window will not lock in closed position
  • Mechanism stripped — can hear grinding
  • Window opens partially then jams

Diagnosis

  1. Remove interior trim piece around operator handle
  2. Inspect gear mechanism — look for stripped gear teeth or broken crank arm
  3. Check if issue is mechanism itself vs track/rail alignment

Parts

  • Pella Standard Operator: PART-OPR-MEC — typically in stock at Savage warehouse
  • Andersen custom operator: order from manufacturer — 5-7 business days
  • Always check warehouse stock before scheduling tech visit

Installation

  1. Remove old operator unit (4 screws typically)
  2. Clean mounting area
  3. Install new operator, test engagement before securing
  4. Test full open/close/lock cycle 3 times before closing up
  5. Apply weatherstrip sealant around trim

Labor Time Estimate

Standard replacement: 45-75 minutes onsite. Add drive time. If rail/track adjustment needed: add 30-45 minutes.

articles.title: Screen Replacement — Measurement, Order, and Installation Guide
articles.description: Standard process for measuring, ordering, and installing replacement screens for windows and doors. Covers frame types and field tech installation steps.
articles.content:
Screen Replacement Process

When to Replace vs Repair

  • Replace frame + screen: Bent or damaged frame, multiple tears, multiple units (easier to batch)
  • Re-screen only: Single tear, frame in good condition — saves cost

Measurement

  1. Measure frame opening (W x H) from inside edge to inside edge of window frame stop
  2. Subtract 1/8 inch from both dimensions for proper fit
  3. Note frame color/finish to match existing hardware
  4. Photograph existing screen and frame for reference

Stock Parts at Savage Warehouse

  • SCR-WIN-STD: Standard sizes 24x36, 24x48, 28x40, 28x48, 30x48, 36x60
  • Custom sizes: 5-7 business day order lead time
  • Check warehouse inventory before scheduling

Installation

  1. Install from outside if possible (better seal)
  2. Seat bottom of frame first, then top, then sides
  3. Check screen tension — should not bow or sag
  4. Test sliding operation if applicable
  5. Capture post-installation photo

articles.title: Parts Order Status — Customer Communication & Self-Service Guide
articles.description: How to check parts order status, what to communicate to customers at each milestone, and how to set up automated status notifications.
articles.content:
Parts Order Status — Communication Guide

Status Milestones and What to Communicate

MilestoneWho Gets NotifiedChannelMessage Type
Parts OrderedHomeowner + ContractorSMS + EmailConfirmation with ETA
7 Days After Order (if not received)HomeownerSMSProactive check-in — parts still on track
Parts Received at WarehouseHomeowner + ContractorSMS + EmailParts arrived — scheduling call coming
Appointment ScheduledHomeownerSMSAppointment confirmation with tech name + time window
Day Before AppointmentHomeownerSMSReminder with option to confirm or reschedule
Tech En RouteHomeownerSMSTech on the way, ETA in minutes
Work CompleteHomeowner + Contractor + Sales RepEmailCompletion report with photos + documentation

Customer Self-Service Options

Customers can check order status via:

  • Calling 952-894-0100 — coordinator can look up in D365 in seconds
  • Email reply to original confirmation — auto-linked to case
  • Future: MyABC Portal or SMS bot reply with case number

articles.title: Field Service Completion Documentation — Photo and Report Requirements
articles.description: What field techs must document at every service visit. Pre-work and post-work photo requirements, inspection checklist, and completion report process.
articles.content:
Field Service Completion Documentation

Required for Every Work Order

  • Pre-work photo (before touching anything)
  • Post-work photo (finished result — same angle as pre)
  • Completed inspection checklist (mobile app)
  • Time log: arrive time, work start, work end, depart time
  • Parts used (scan or select from mobile)

Photo Requirements

  • Pre-work: Shows existing condition — damage, issue area, measurement reference
  • Post-work: Shows completed installation/repair — same angle to allow comparison
  • Additional: Any unusual findings, code concerns, or items requiring follow-up

Inspection Checklist Items (Standard Window/Door Visit)

  1. Frame condition — no cracks, warping, or damage
  2. Glass condition — no scratches, cracks, or seal failure
  3. Seal/weatherstrip — intact and properly seated
  4. Hardware operation — locks, hinges, or operators function correctly
  5. Installation plumb and level
  6. Interior trim/finish — no damage from installation
  7. Customer walkthrough completed — customer satisfaction confirmed

Completion Report

D365 Field Service auto-generates completion report on case close. Report includes: work order details, tech name and contact, parts used, labor time, pre/post photos, inspection checklist results. Auto-emailed to contractor (primary contact) and homeowner (if linked). Sales rep also CC'd per account settings.
